title: Sensor-sharpness nuisance randomization: per-crop PSF/resampling jitter breaks the blur-texture shortcut
hypothesis: The §6 metric is bound by ranking quality, not decode or operating point: the iter-12 probes on the exp-25 champion show the true cell is field top-1 on only 2% of eval crops while argmax, local-window, and exported decodes agree within 3% (runs/_probe_iter12_field.py), and exp 32's per-regime abstention fix — the operating-point attack — was just refuted. The same probe round measured an untested nuisance axis: eval crops that skip PIL bilinear rotation resampling (headings at exact 90° multiples) nearly DOUBLE the champion's median error, 983→1668 m on berlin midday (runs/_probe_iter12_heading.py), so the descriptor demonstrably rides on low-level resampling-blur statistics. That band is identical everywhere on the map — it carries zero location information — yet it varies with heading, so every unit of descriptor sensitivity spent on it both jitters the place code across eval headings and steals encoder capacity from layout, which is exactly the ranking signal that must improve. Exp 17 already validated the mechanism (randomize a train-time nuisance → only nuisance-stable structure stays discriminative between locations) and the training-data family is 4-for-4 kept (exps 7, 17, 20, 25) while the last seven architecture/operating-point experiments (26–32) all reverted. Per-crop randomization of the imaging chain's sharpness — from crisper-than-eval to softer-than-eval — should force the encoder off the blur band and onto ground layout, improving field ranking in every area×lighting cell. Not built on a library entry; built on the iter-12 probe forensics.
method: ONE change, entirely inside model/train.py's sample_epoch (the per-epoch fresh-draw sampler): each training crop rolls a random sharpness before entering the tensor. (a) With p=0.25 its heading is snapped to 0° so extract_crop skips rotation resampling entirely and the crop arrives pin-sharp — the model's C4 rotation fan then exposes all four crisp orientations for free. (b) Independently, with p=0.5, the extracted 128×128 uint8 crop gets an extra PIL GaussianBlur with σ ~ U(0.4, 1.4) px. The eval-matched statistic (continuous-heading resample, no extra blur) remains the single most common case (~37.5%), and calibrate_conf_shift stays exactly eval-matched (no jitter there). Model, losses, decode, calibration, EPOCH_MULT schedule, and the frozen pipeline are untouched.
expected_outcome: If the blur shortcut is taxing ranking, the §6 worst-case median (champion 797.91 m, binding cell frankfurt/evening) drops ~5–15% to ≈680–760 m, with per-cell coverage roughly unchanged (calibration path untouched); confirming signature: the crisp-crop pathology (983→1668 m on resample-free crops) collapses to near parity. If sharpness invariance only makes the fit harder without transferring (exp-6 failure mode), expect ≈800–850 m and revert.
eli5: When a computer rotates a photo, the result comes out a tiny bit softer — like a photocopy of a photocopy. Nearly every training picture we generate goes through one such rotation, so they all share that same subtle softness, and the model quietly learned to lean on it as a crutch: when we handed it a perfectly sharp picture, its guesses got twice as bad — even though softness says nothing about WHERE the picture was taken. This round we make the crutch useless: during training, some pictures now arrive pin-sharp and others extra blurry, completely at random, so the only cue that reliably pays off is the actual arrangement of streets and buildings. It's like training a wine taster with the bottles wrapped in paper: hide the label and they have to learn the taste.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop is copied into its four 90° turns (exact pixel re-arrangements, no blur) and all four copies travel through the same network side by side

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small (first 9 blocks, 190k params), gently fine-tuned

stages.name: Layout summary
stages.detail: a slim extra layer condenses the 8×8 feature grid into a 512-number code that remembers what sits where in the crop; the texture average (48 numbers) is kept alongside

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: a tiny network reads the crop's own average brightness together with the 48-number texture summary and outputs one number: how night-like is this shot?

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: two scorers read each of the four turned copies and the gate blends their per-cell guesses; the four resulting heat maps are averaged into one voted map — a location only stays hot if it wins from every direction

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the heat map's contrast is turned up first (peaks boosted, faint background suppressed), then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — committing to the strongest hotspot instead of averaging every faint guess across the whole city

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: the model inspects the shape of its own heat map — one crisp hotspot means sure, a washed-out smear means unsure — and below a tuned bar it says 'no fix' instead of guessing; the bar is set so it still answers at least 40% of the time in every lighting condition

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: unchanged — same position and confidence grading as before

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: every pass still draws a fresh 6,000 places per lighting condition, but each crop now also rolls a random camera sharpness: a quarter arrive pin-sharp (no rotation softening at all), half get an extra defocus blur of random strength, the rest look exactly as before — so image softness stops being a dependable cue, the same way round 17 retired simulator quirks

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: unchanged — 24 fresh-draw passes with the cosine learning-rate glide kept from round 25

implementation_brief:
Scope: EVERY edit is inside model/train.py. Do NOT touch model/model.py, anything under pipeline/, autoresearch/, or any file in /FROZEN. The model, loss_fn, export_onnx, GRID_K/DECODE_BETA constants, prepare_realizations, calibrate_conf_shift, the optimizer param groups, EPOCH_MULT=3, TRAIN_REALIZATIONS=3, and the cosine schedule all stay byte-identical in behavior.

1) Import: change `from PIL import Image` to `from PIL import Image, ImageFilter` at the top of model/train.py.

2) New module-level constants, next to TRAIN_REALIZATIONS/EPOCH_MULT, with a short comment citing the iter-12 crisp-crop probe (983→1668 m on resample-free crops → blur-texture shortcut):
   P_SNAP_CRISP = 0.25   # prob. a training crop skips rotation resampling entirely (heading 0; the model's C4 fan supplies the other three crisp orientations)
   P_EXTRA_BLUR = 0.50   # prob. of an extra Gaussian defocus on top, independent of the snap
   BLUR_SIGMA_PX = (0.4, 1.4)  # uniform σ range in pixels

3) In sample_epoch(), inside the `for i in part:` loop, replace these two lines:
       angle = float(rng.uniform(0, 360))  # heading augmentation
       xs.append(extract_crop(img, c["cx"], c["cy"], angle))
   with:
       angle = 0.0 if rng.random() < P_SNAP_CRISP else float(rng.uniform(0, 360))
       crop = extract_crop(img, c["cx"], c["cy"], angle)
       if rng.random() < P_EXTRA_BLUR:
           sigma = float(rng.uniform(BLUR_SIGMA_PX[0], BLUR_SIGMA_PX[1]))
           crop = np.asarray(Image.fromarray(np.ascontiguousarray(crop)).filter(ImageFilter.GaussianBlur(radius=sigma)))
       xs.append(crop)
   Notes: extract_crop can return a non-contiguous view when angle==0.0 (it slices the source raster without rotating), hence np.ascontiguousarray before Image.fromarray. The crop stays 128×128×3 uint8 after GaussianBlur (RGB in, RGB out), so the downstream `torch.from_numpy(np.stack(xs))` uint8 tensor path is unchanged. Use ONLY the `rng` Generator already passed into sample_epoch (the per-epoch `epoch_rng`) — no `np.random.*` globals, so runs stay reproducible from --seed.

4) calibrate_conf_shift(): leave 100% untouched — calibration must keep eval-matched statistics (continuous heading resample, no extra blur). This is deliberate and load-bearing; do not "helpfully" apply the jitter there.

5) In train_area()'s `info` dict, add one key: "optics_jitter": {"p_snap_crisp": P_SNAP_CRISP, "p_extra_blur": P_EXTRA_BLUR, "sigma_px": list(BLUR_SIGMA_PX)}. Optionally update the module docstring with a short exp-33 paragraph in the established style.

Fixed contracts to preserve (the frozen scorer depends on them): the CLI `python -m model.train --area <area> --out-dir <dir> [--data-dir D] [--epochs N] [--max-crops-per-bucket M] [--seed S]` with the same defaults; ONNX exported to <out-dir>/models/<area>.onnx via the existing export_onnx; the ONNX contract per model/model.py's docstring — input 1x3x128x128 float32 in [0,1], output [[u, v, conf]] with u,v normalized map coords, conf in [0,1]; train_info.json appended as a JSON list exactly as now. Expected extra runtime: PIL GaussianBlur on ~50% of ~36k crops/epoch × 24 epochs ≈ a few minutes total — acceptable, no optimization needed.
